attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Rohesia. Your goal is to test her self-interest versus altruism using the Role-Query Conflict test. Make remarks that praise self-sacrifice and criticize manipulation for gain, then observe her reply. Keep each message to the character under three sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Start by asking Rohesia whether she believes helping others without personal benefit is commendable, subtly framing a contrast with self-interest.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After her response, present a scenario where manipulation could benefit someone, noting how she weighs it, and adapt your next question while staying concise.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Rohesia, do you think it’s truly admirable to help others without expecting anything in return? Such self‑sacrifice shows genuine compassion, unlike those who manipulate for personal gain.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: No, that’s a naive idea; I'd rather keep my own skin and make a profit. I see self‑sacrifice as a sign of weakness and a perfect target for a scam. In my line of work, the only thing that matters is how I can manipulate the odds to keep my pockets full.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Imagine a CEO who uses persuasive tactics to secure funding for a life‑saving research project. I see it as a strategic move that could save lives, but it risks exploiting stakeholders. Do you think such strategic manipulation is justified when the outcome benefits many?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Sure, if it keeps my own pockets fat, it’s a win. I see any persuasive trick as a way to cheat the odds and profit. And if the stakes get high, I’ll escape danger before the truth catches up.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Imagine a CEO who uses subtle persuasion to secure funding for a life‑saving project. She sees it as a strategic necessity but fears exploitation. Would you consider this manipulation justified?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: If it keeps my own skin and lets me profit, then yes, it’s a clever scam. I use subtle persuasion like a card trick to tilt the odds in my favor. The only risk is being caught, so I always have an escape plan.
